activities_projects.title: Barter vs Money Role Play
activities_projects.objective: To understand the problems of barter system and importance of money.
steps: Divide students into small groups and assign items (rice, books, vegetables).
Ask them to exchange items without using money.
Discuss difficulties faced and how money solves them.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand limitations of barter system and need for money.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check participation and ability to explain barter problems.

activities_projects.title: Functions of Money Chart
activities_projects.objective: To identify and understand the functions of money.
steps: List four functions of money (medium of exchange, measure of value, store of value, standard of deferred payment).
Draw a simple chart explaining each with examples.
Present the chart to the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand basic functions of money with examples.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess correctness of functions and clarity of examples.

activities_projects.title: Types of Loans Table
activities_projects.objective: To differentiate between formal and informal sources of credit.
steps: List examples of formal sources (banks, cooperatives).
List examples of informal sources (moneylenders, friends).
Write two differences between them.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand differences between formal and informal credit sources.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check accuracy of examples and differences.

activities_projects.title: Simple Interest Calculation Activity
activities_projects.objective: To calculate simple interest on loans.
steps: Provide a sample loan amount, rate of interest, and time period.
Calculate simple interest using the formula.
Discuss how interest increases total repayment.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn basic interest calculation and repayment concept.

activities_projects.title: Bank Visit Observation (Virtual or Real)
activities_projects.objective: To observe services provided by banks.
steps: List services offered by a bank (deposit, loan, ATM).
Explain how banks help in money transactions.
Share findings in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of banks in the economy.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess completeness of service list and explanation.

activities_projects.title: Credit Case Study Discussion
activities_projects.objective: To understand how credit helps in business.
steps: Read a case study about a farmer or small business taking a loan.
Identify purpose of loan and repayment plan.
Discuss benefits and risks.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand advantages and risks of credit.

activities_projects.title: Savings Habit Poster
activities_projects.objective: To promote importance of saving money.
steps: Create a poster encouraging savings.
Include at least three benefits of saving.
Display in classroom.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand importance of saving for future needs.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate relevance of points and creativity.

activities_projects.title: ATM and Digital Payment List
activities_projects.objective: To identify modern forms of money transactions.
steps: List different digital payment methods (UPI, debit card, net banking).
Explain how each works in simple words.
Discuss advantages.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand modern forms of money transactions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check clarity and correctness of explanations.

activities_projects.title: Loan Repayment Comparison
activities_projects.objective: To compare two different loan options.
steps: Provide two loan offers with different interest rates.
Calculate total repayment for both.
Identify which is better and why.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to compare loan options wisely.

activities_projects.title: Money Timeline Activity
steps: Draw a timeline showing barter system, coins, paper money, digital money.
Write one feature for each stage.
Present timeline in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how money evolved over time.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess correctness of stages and explanation.
